raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Misclassification of Returned Payment as Purchase and Denial of Fee Waiver Complaint Summary : Im filing this complaint regarding how Citibank handled a recent issue on my credit card account. On or around XX/XX/year>, a scheduled payment of {$650.00} failed due to an error on my part. I had unintentionally unlinked my Citi account from my XXXXXXXX XXXX account, resulting in an invalid payment method. 

Citi attempted to pull the payment three times and ultimately marked it as a returned payment. However, instead of simply flagging it as a failed transaction, Citi classified this failed payment as a purchase. That shifted the balance into a segment subject to a different APR than my existing balance transfer promotional offer. 

This is a misrepresentation of the transaction type. No purchase ever occurred, as this was a failed ACH payment attempt. Moreover, the funds were never actually pulled from my bank. Labeling it a returned payment that adds to my purchase balance and incurs interest is both misleading and financially punitive. 

I promptly corrected the issue by making a new payment that successfully posted. I requested a one-time courtesy waiver for the {$30.00} returned payment fee, but was told by multiple Citi representatives , including a supervisor, that the system denied the request and could not be overridden. This was despite my account being in otherwise good standing and the situation being an honest, corrected mistake.

Key Issues : - A returned payment was misclassified as a purchase, which affected promotional APR terms - Citis system is charging additional interest on a non-purchase, which appears to violate the intent of the original offer - The bank refused a reasonable one-time fee waiver despite a good payment history and prompt resolution - There was no fair or effective escalation path, and I was told there was nothing anyone could do I believe this practice is deceptive and deserves regulatory review.
